label: Positional, temporal & coordinate encodings

node.label: Positional, temporal & coordinate encodings
node.definition: Coordinates, phases, or learned positions inject order, time, or location into models whose base computation is otherwise permutation-equivariant or location-agnostic.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Positional, temporal & coordinate encodings is to connect Sequences, events & time series, Embeddings & distributed representations to downstream capabilities including Relative position, RoPE, ALiBi & coordinate mechanisms, Neural fields & radiance-field models.
examples: substrate for Relative position, RoPE, ALiBi & coordinate mechanisms
substrate for Neural fields & radiance-field models
limitations: Encoding choices can discard information, amplify bias, or make important invariances difficult to learn. Its direct dependencies include Sequences, events & time series, Embeddings & distributed representations.

dependencies.rationale: Positional, temporal & coordinate encodings consumes, emits, or organizes information using Sequences, events & time series.
Positional, temporal & coordinate encodings consumes, emits, or organizes information using Embeddings & distributed representations.
Positional, temporal & coordinate encodings rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Geometry, topology & symmetry.
